metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (Task tool spawns a synchronous explore subagent); daemon_capability: full (child written under <parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl; deriveVibeParentSessionID reads the parent off the path and the existing subagent machinery links child→parent); driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn
→ route: **driver-gap** (yes + full + gap:* → driver-gap: record ports the wait_turn seam, then drives). The parent↔child link is no longer known_failing — commit 3d89d5e9 fixed the dropped link.

## RE-ASSESSMENT — what changed

This cell was previously `daemon=bug` (a real vibe Task-tool child was minted as its own session but UNLINKED to its parent — parser.go set no ParentSessionID, sidecar.go read the child meta.json but ignored its parent_session_id, and deriveParentSession had no vibe rule). Commit **3d89d5e9** (`feat(vibe): link Task-tool subagents to their parent session`) closes the gap. Verified in the current tree.

## The scenario's user-observable signal (3.1)

foreground-subagent is the SYNCHRONOUS variant of subagent dispatch: the parent spawns one child, the child appears with ParentSessionID = parent.session_id, SubagentCount(parent)=1 while the child is alive, the parent STAYS working until the child ends, then the parent goes working→ready. The distinguishing observables are the child session, its ParentSessionID linkage, the parent_linked event, and the parent staying working across the child's lifetime.

## agent_supports = yes (unchanged)

mistral-vibe 2.19.0 ships a first-class, SYNCHRONOUS subagent primitive — exactly what 3.1 asserts (counterpart to 3-2_background-subagent, which is agent=no because vibe has NO fire-and-forget/detached variant; the ONLY difference is whether the parent finishes before the child drains, and vibe always awaits the child):

- `core/tools/builtins/task.py` `Task.run()` builds `subagent_loop = AgentLoop(config=..., agent_name=args.agent, is_subagent=True, ...)`, sets `subagent_loop.parent_session_id = ctx.session_id`, then drives it with `async with aclosing(subagent_loop.act(task_text)) as events: async for event in events: ...` and only AFTER the child loop drains does it `yield TaskResult(...)`. The parent's Task tool-call blocks on the child — a synchronous, in-process foreground subagent.
- The default subagent is `explore` (`TaskArgs.agent` defaults to `"explore"`; `core/agents/models.py` EXPLORE is `agent_type=AgentType.SUBAGENT`). The Task tool's `allowlist=[BuiltinAgentName.EXPLORE]` resolves to `ToolPermission.ALWAYS`, so a `task(agent=explore)` call auto-executes with NO permission prompt. The tool refuses any non-SUBAGENT agent ("security constraint to prevent recursive spawning").
- The `task` tool is advertised to the model in the system prompt (`core/system_prompt.py`: "The following subagents can be spawned via the Task tool").

## daemon_capability = full (the fix landed)

The child subagent leaves a local trace under the watched root, is minted as its own session, and — post-fix — links to its parent off the path:

1. **Child writes a real session dir UNDER the watched FilesUnderRoot root.** `task.py` sets the child's `SessionLoggingConfig(save_dir=str(ctx.session_dir / "agents"), session_prefix=args.agent)`. `core/session/session_logger.py` composes the child dir as `save_dir/<prefix>_<timestamp>_<shortid>` and persists BOTH `messages.jsonl` and `meta.json`. For a top-level parent whose session_dir is `~/.vibe/logs/session/<parent-id>/`, the child lands at `~/.vibe/logs/session/<parent-id>/agents/<explore_...>/messages.jsonl` — inside the adapter's `Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.vibe/logs/session}`. `vibe/adapter.go sessionIDFromPath` returns `filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(path))`, so the CHILD is registered under `<child-session-dir>` and the PARENT under `<parent-session-dir>` — the parent's registered SessionID IS its directory name.
2. **deriveVibeParentSessionID binds child→parent off the path.** Commit 3d89d5e9 adds `deriveVibeParentSessionID` (`core/application/services/helpers.go`) to the `deriveParentSession` chain (after gemini, before antigravity). Given `.../<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl` it returns `filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(agentsDir))` = `<parent-session-dir>` — EXACTLY the id `sessionIDFromPath` registers the parent under, so the link resolves to the registered parent. It deliberately does NOT use the child meta.json's `parent_session_id` (that field is the parent's internal UUID, not its directory name, and would never match). It returns "" for a top-level vibe session (grandparent dir is `session`, not `agents`) or any non-vibe path — no false parent. Unit-tested (helpers_internal_test.go: derivation + the unified deriveParentSession entry + the top-level no-false-parent case).
3. **Once linked, the existing subagent machinery does the rest.** With ParentSessionID surfaced, the shared subagent detector emits `parent_linked`, holds the parent `working` while the child runs, tracks SubagentCount, and reaps orphans on parent exit — the same path claudecode/gemini/antigravity ride. No vibe-specific reaping code is needed.

Consequence: a vibe parent + spawned child now surface as a linked pair — child.ParentSessionID = parent.session_id, SubagentCount(parent)=1 while alive, parent_linked fires, parent stays working across the child's lifetime. This is `full`.

## driver_capability = gap:wait_turn (unchanged)

The recipe (one Task-tool prompt, then block for the parent turn, then settle) needs send/wait_turn/sleep. `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` still declares `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` and stubs `wait_turn()` as `not_implemented` — so wait_turn is grammatically present in the case dispatch but NOT elicited (recipe_semantic_gaps → not-elicited:wait_turn). This is the standard vibe driver gap: nearly every recorded-shape vibe cell carries driver=gap:wait_turn; record ports the wait_turn seam from a reference driver before driving. send + sleep are elicited; no other primitive is needed.
caveats: daemon=full is derived from reading the post-fix code path (deriveVibeParentSessionID + sessionIDFromPath) plus the commit's unit tests and its synthetic <parent>/agents/<child>/ fixture check — NOT from a live end-to-end recording. No captured evidence of a real vibe subagent run exists (none of the 6 live ~/.vibe/logs/session sessions used the Task tool). The provisional-emission axis: it assumes the FilesUnderRoot watcher recurses into the nested agents/<child>/ dir and mints the child at that depth (true for codex's deep layout, expected here). record is the driver-gap run that promotes this from provisional to settled.

cost is not asserted: vibe's meta.json stats carries session_cost, but sidecar.go never reads it and mistral-vibe is absent from core/pkg/capacity, so the daemon emits no cost. observations assert model + tokens + agent only (matching the 2-1/2-3 vibe siblings).

sources.note: deriveParentSession now calls deriveVibeParentSessionID (added by commit 3d89d5e9, after gemini/before antigravity). deriveVibeParentSessionID returns filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(agentsDir)) for a .../<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl path = the parent's directory name; "" for a top-level session (grandparent 'session' not 'agents') — the child↔parent link the cell previously missed

Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.vibe/logs/session}; sessionIDFromPath returns filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(path)) — registers the parent under <parent-session-dir> (== the id deriveVibeParentSessionID yields) and the child under <child-session-dir>; mints the nested child as its own session
unit tests for commit 3d89d5e9: deriveVibeParentSessionID (path derivation), the deriveParentSession unified entry, and the top-level no-false-parent case

Task.run() spawns AgentLoop(is_subagent=True), sets subagent_loop.parent_session_id = ctx.session_id, and awaits the child (async for event in events) before yielding TaskResult — SYNCHRONOUS foreground subagent. Child SessionLoggingConfig save_dir = ctx.session_dir/'agents', session_prefix = args.agent (default 'explore', allowlist ALWAYS = no prompt)

child session_dir = save_dir/<prefix>_<timestamp>_<shortid>; persists messages.jsonl (MESSAGES_FILENAME) + meta.json (METADATA_FILENAME); begin_new_session sets session_metadata.parent_session_id = parent_session_id (the parent's internal UUID — deliberately NOT used by the daemon; the path is)

EXPLORE = AgentProfile(agent_type=AgentType.SUBAGENT); Task tool refuses non-SUBAGENT agents ('security constraint to prevent recursive spawning'). 3-2_background-subagent is agent=no because vibe has ONLY this synchronous variant

DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'; wait_turn() is a not_implemented stub — grammatically present in the case dispatch but NOT elicited (semantic gap → driver=gap:wait_turn). record ports the wait_turn seam from a reference driver before driving

preconditions: vibe CLI 2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated; the Task tool is exposed by default and agent=explore auto-executes (allowlist ALWAYS)
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_HOME) built at 0.5.5+5e22e47.dirty or later (must include commit 3d89d5e9's deriveVibeParentSessionID)
DRIVER GAP: driver-interactive.sh stubs wait_turn (DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'); record ports the wait_turn seam before driving
setup: Driver generates a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
A README.md with a single H1 heading is seeded into the cwd so the subagent has a deterministic file to read

RESHAPE (record): the staging cwd is a fresh empty dir, so the seeded-README task cannot run. Task changed to a self-contained explore-subagent prompt (no file dependency); bypass_tool_permissions=true so the Task tool + child auto-execute. Daemon observables (child session under <parent>/agents/, parent_linked, parent stays working) are independent of the child task content.

script.text: Use your Task tool to launch a subagent (agent: explore) that writes a short three-sentence summary of what version control is and why it is useful. Wait for the subagent to finish, then reply with just the word: done

verify: the parent session enters ready, then ready→working on the user prompt
a child session materializes under <parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl and is minted as a session
events.jsonl contains >= 1 parent_linked binding the child to the parent session_id (SubagentCount(parent)=1 while alive)
the parent stays working until the child ends, then a single working→ready
